text:
Once upon a time in a small town named Whimperwick, there lived a boy named Benny. Benny had always dreamed of flying like a bird. He had a small attic studio where he painted pictures about birds flapping around.

One sunny afternoon, while Benny was painting a picture, his pet parakeet, Peety, flew into a featherbed filled with dreams. Peety fluttered around, casting shadows on Bernice’s pillow cases, and finally landed on Benny’s painting.

Benny looked at the parakeet on his artwork, a blue-feathered thing with sparkling eye sockets. He loved Peety. Peety pecked on the painting, then drifted down to rest on Benny’s cheek.

Later that day, a gust of wind blew through Whimperwick’s park, scattering thousands of snowflakes. Benny saw a snowflake fall just beside Peety. He reached up, caught it delicately, and brought it inside.
